stimulus.content: Congress passed a new law. The President did not agree with the law, so the President vetoed it. Congress then voted again, and two-thirds of the members voted to pass the law anyway.

stem: What does this example best show about the United States government?

choices.text: The President has the final say on all laws
Congress can never pass a law the President rejects
The branches of government can check each other's power
Only the judicial branch can decide if a law is fair

answer_explanation: This is an example of checks and balances. The President checked Congress by using the veto. Congress then checked the President by overriding the veto with a two-thirds vote. This shows how the branches limit each other's power.
